output:
Aggregated execute batches: completed 6/6.
Batch 1: Executed batch 1 of 6 for T1 only. Patched `src/tools/video-editor/components/PreviewPanel/OverlayEditor.tsx` so the resize-direction `includes()` checks run only when `state.mode !== 'move'`, which prevents move drags from mutating width/height. Verified SC1 by inspecting the guarded `else` branch and checkpointed the batch result to `.megaplan/plans/fix-the-video-editor-s-20260325-1831/execution_batch_1.json`.
Batch 2: Executed batch 2 of 6 for T2 and T3 only. Updated `src/tools/video-editor/components/PreviewPanel/OverlayEditor.tsx` so drag state now captures `scaleX`/`scaleY` at drag start and applies them to mouse deltas during move/resize, and removed the dashed `dragOriginStyle` origin layer that was causing the preview ghost artifact. Checkpointed the batch result to `.megaplan/plans/fix-the-video-editor-s-20260325-1831/execution_batch_2.json`.
Batch 3: Executed batch 3 of 6 for T4 only. Added crop fields to the clip type/model, both timeline-data load/save mappings, the serialized clip whitelist, and the reset-position callback so crop values can round-trip and be cleared correctly. Checkpointed the batch result to `.megaplan/plans/fix-the-video-editor-s-20260325-1831/execution_batch_3.json`.
Batch 4: Executed batch 4 of 6 for T5 and T7 only. `OverlayEditor` now supports crop edge handles with dedicated crop drag modes, crop-aware visible bounds, clamped crop math, and crop-only commits, while `VisualClip` now applies crop through `clipPath: inset(top right bottom left)`. Verified with direct code inspection and a clean `npm exec tsc --noEmit`, then checkpointed the batch to `.megaplan/plans/fix-the-video-editor-s-20260325-1831/execution_batch_4.json`.
Batch 5: Executed batch 5 of 6 for T6 only. `OverlayEditor.tsx` now renders a dimmed dashed ghost of the full uncropped bounds behind the visible cropped overlay when the clip is selected and has non-zero crop, and the effective overlay model keeps `fullBounds` current during drag. Verified with direct code inspection and a clean `npm exec tsc --noEmit`, then checkpointed the batch to `.megaplan/plans/fix-the-video-editor-s-20260325-1831/execution_batch_5.json`.
Batch 6: Executed batch 6 of 6 for T8 only. Validation was completed on a best-effort basis in this environment: TypeScript compilation passed, crop load/save was runtime-validated through a `configToRows`/`rowsToConfig` round-trip, reset behavior and existing interaction paths were re-inspected in code, and the batch result was checkpointed to `.megaplan/plans/fix-the-video-editor-s-20260325-1831/execution_batch_6.json`. Manual pointer-driven preview interaction checks and a visual Remotion preview check were not performed in this headless sandbox.
commands_run: npm exec tsx --eval "import { configToRows, rowsToConfig } from './src/tools/video-editor/lib/timeline-data.ts'; const config = { output: { resolution: '1920x1080', fps: 30, file: 'out.mp4' }, tracks: [{ id: 'V1', kind: 'visual', label: 'Track 1' }], clips: [{ id: 'clip1', at: 0, track: 'V1', asset: 'asset-1', from: 0, to: 2, cropTop: 0.1, cropRight: 0.2, cropBottom: 0.15, cropLeft: 0.05 }] }; const { rows, meta, clipOrder } = configToRows(config as any); const roundTripped = rowsToConfig(rows, meta, config.output as any, clipOrder, config.tracks as any); console.log(JSON.stringify({ meta: meta['clip1'], clip: roundTripped.clips[0] }, null, 2));"

node --import tsx/esm --input-type=module -e "import { configToRows, rowsToConfig } from './src/tools/video-editor/lib/timeline-data.ts'; const config = { output: { resolution: '1920x1080', fps: 30, file: 'out.mp4' }, tracks: [{ id: 'V1', kind: 'visual', label: 'Track 1' }], clips: [{ id: 'clip1', at: 0, track: 'V1', asset: 'asset-1', from: 0, to: 2, cropTop: 0.1, cropRight: 0.2, cropBottom: 0.15, cropLeft: 0.05 }] }; const { rows, meta, clipOrder } = configToRows(config); const roundTripped = rowsToConfig(rows, meta, config.output, clipOrder, config.tracks); console.log(JSON.stringify({ meta: meta['clip1'], clip: roundTripped.clips[0] }, null, 2));"

task_updates.executor_notes: Wrapped the directional resize checks in an `else` after the `move` branch in `OverlayEditor.tsx`, so move drags now update only `x` and `y`. Verified the `e/s/w/n` `includes()` branches are all guarded by that `else`, preventing width/height changes during move.

Stored `scaleX` and `scaleY` in the drag state at drag start and now multiply screen-pixel mouse deltas by those factors before mutating composition-space bounds. Verified the scale is derived from `compositionWidth / layout.width` and `compositionHeight / layout.height` in `startDrag`, then consumed in `onMouseMove`, which is the required fix for consistent cursor-to-overlay movement when the preview is scaled.

Removed the `dragOriginStyle` computation and the dashed-border origin `<div>` from the overlay container. Verified there are no remaining `dragOriginStyle` references in `OverlayEditor.tsx`, so the start-position ghost layer is no longer rendered during move or resize.

Added `cropTop`, `cropBottom`, `cropLeft`, and `cropRight` to the `TimelineClip` type, the `ClipMeta` interface, the clip-to-meta load mapping in `getDefaultClipMeta`, the meta-to-clip save mapping in `rowsToConfig`, and the `TIMELINE_CLIP_FIELDS` whitelist. Also updated `handleResetClipPosition` to clear all four crop fields so reset removes crop as well as position/size overrides. Verified each required site directly after patching.

Extended `OverlayEditor` with crop drag modes, crop-aware overlay view models, crop state in the drag ref, and non-text edge handles for `crop-n/s/e/w`. The overlay now renders cropped visible bounds from the underlying full clip bounds, crop drags clamp each axis so opposite edges cannot overlap, and crop-mode commits send only crop fields while move/resize commits convert the visible rectangle back to full `x/y/width/height`. Verified by direct code inspection and a clean `npm exec tsc --noEmit`.

Updated `getClipBoxStyle` in `VisualClip.tsx` to apply crop via CSS `clipPath: inset(top right bottom left)` using the stored crop fractions. This keeps Remotion preview/render output aligned with the crop model introduced in the editor. Verified the inset order directly in code and with a clean `npm exec tsc --noEmit`.

Updated `OverlayEditor.tsx` so effective overlays carry an up-to-date `fullBounds` value during drag, and selected overlays with non-zero crop now render a dimmed dashed ghost of the full uncropped bounds behind the visible cropped rectangle. Verified the ghost is gated by `isSelected && hasCrop`, rendered before the actual overlay control for correct z-order, and therefore disappears automatically on deselection. TypeScript compile check passed with `npm exec tsc --noEmit`.

Validation completed on a best-effort basis in this environment. Confirmed by `./node_modules/.bin/tsc --noEmit` that the changed files still typecheck. Confirmed by a runtime `node --import tsx/esm` round-trip exercise that crop fields load into editor meta and serialize back out unchanged through `configToRows`/`rowsToConfig`, which also re-runs `validateSerializedConfig`. Confirmed by code inspection that `handleResetClipPosition` clears both position and crop fields, that text editing via double-click and inline textarea paths remain present, that selection and corner resize/move paths still exist alongside the new crop paths, and that selected cropped clips render the full-bounds ghost. Manual pointer-driven UI validation and a visual Remotion preview check were not performed in this headless sandbox, so those specific UX items remain code-inspected rather than manually exercised.
commands_run: node --import tsx/esm --input-type=module -e "import { configToRows, rowsToConfig } from './src/tools/video-editor/lib/timeline-data.ts'; const config = { output: { resolution: '1920x1080', fps: 30, file: 'out.mp4' }, tracks: [{ id: 'V1', kind: 'visual', label: 'Track 1' }], clips: [{ id: 'clip1', at: 0, track: 'V1', asset: 'asset-1', from: 0, to: 2, cropTop: 0.1, cropRight: 0.2, cropBottom: 0.15, cropLeft: 0.05 }] }; const { rows, meta, clipOrder } = configToRows(config); const roundTripped = rowsToConfig(rows, meta, config.output, clipOrder, config.tracks); console.log(JSON.stringify({ meta: meta['clip1'], clip: roundTripped.clips[0] }, null, 2));"

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: Confirmed from the patched handler that `state.mode === 'move'` changes only `nextBounds.x` and `nextBounds.y`, while all four resize-direction checks (`e/s/w/n`) now run only inside the `else`. Width and height therefore remain unchanged during move.

Confirmed by code inspection that drag deltas are now converted from screen pixels into composition units using `scaleX = compositionWidth / layout.width` and `scaleY = compositionHeight / layout.height`, captured in the drag ref at drag start and applied on every mousemove. That is the intended 1:1 cursor-tracking fix across different preview sizes.

Confirmed the dashed origin ghost layer is removed: the `dragOriginStyle` object no longer exists and there is no conditional `<div>` rendering it in the overlay container. Only the active overlay remains during drag, so no visual remnant is left at the start position.

Confirmed the crop fields exist in all required persistence and reset locations: `TimelineClip`, `ClipMeta`, `getDefaultClipMeta`, the `rowsToConfig` serialization mapping, and `TIMELINE_CLIP_FIELDS`, with `handleResetClipPosition` also clearing all four crop keys. This closes both load/save directions and the validation gate.

Confirmed from the patched `OverlayEditor` that edge handles render only under `!overlay.isText`, crop drags go through a dedicated `state.mode.startsWith('crop-')` branch instead of the resize path, and crop-mode commits update only crop fields. The clamp upper bounds use the opposite crop value plus a minimum visible fraction, so `cropLeft + cropRight` and `cropTop + cropBottom` cannot reach or exceed 1.

Confirmed `VisualClip` now applies `style.clipPath = inset(cropTop cropRight cropBottom cropLeft)` in top-right-bottom-left order. That means `cropLeft = 0.1` produces a `10%` left inset, which removes 10% from the left side in Remotion preview/render. The code typechecks cleanly with `npm exec tsc --noEmit`.

Confirmed from the patched `OverlayEditor` that the full-bounds ghost is rendered only when `selectedClipId === overlay.actionId` and at least one crop value is non-zero. The ghost layer is inserted before the main overlay control inside the same positioned container, so it sits behind the cropped overlay in z-order and disappears automatically when the clip is deselected.

Verified by runtime round-trip that crop persists through load/save, and by code inspection that `handleResetClipPosition` clears both position and crop fields. Re-inspected the `OverlayEditor` interaction paths for move, corner resize, clip selection, and text double-click/editing, and confirmed the changed code typechecks cleanly. Manual pointer/visual validation for the preview interactions and Remotion player output was not performed in this environment, so those specific UX checks remain unverified by direct interaction.
